description: Letter from a certain Asad to a certain Avraham. In Hebrew and Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: Probably 13th century. He conveys his longing (including kissing the addressee's letter). He explains that he sent the quires (karārīs) for Sayyidnā with his father, and they were only delayed because he could not find a messenger. He wants al-Shaykh al-Makīn ('my teacher') to send the recipe for the collyrium (ashyāfa) known as al-maktūma (or al-makhtūma). On verso there are also some adages in Arabic script (also attested in literary sources), including "He who knows that he will be forgotten when he dies, let him act well and not be forgotten."
description_he: מכתב מאסד לאברהם מסוים. בעברית וביהודית-ערבית. תאריך: ככל הנראה המאה ה-13. מבטא כמיהה. שלח קוויות (כרארים) ל'סידנא'. מבקש מהמורה מתכון לקולירים (אשיאפה) הנקרא 'אל-מכתומה'. גב: פתגמים בכתב ערבי.
